subject: Yoruba Language
context: Understanding the three types of Yoruba tones: middle tone (ohun aarin - unmarked), high tone (ohun oke - marked with acute accent), and low tone (ohun ile - marked with grave accent)

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Learning to mark tone marks on Yoruba vowels correctly, such as à, a, á, è, é, e, ẹ̀, ẹ, ẹ́, understanding how different tones change word meanings

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Applying tone marks correctly on monosyllabic words (single syllable words) and understanding how tone affects pronunciation and meaning, examples: bá, ba, bà, dà, dá, da

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Ami Ohun lori Oro Olopo Silebu
context: Marking tones on polysyllabic words (multiple syllable words) including words with nasal consonants like ọkọ, okó, òkò, pátápátá, pàtàpàtà, kelekèle, àgbagbà

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Learning to read and write numbers in Yoruba from one thousand (egberun) to two thousand (egbaa), understanding the number system and converting between Yoruba words and figures

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Isori Oro - Oruko ati Aropo Oruko
context: Understanding word classes in Yoruba: nouns (oro oruko) and pronouns (oro aropo oruko), their positions and functions in sentences

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Learning about adjectives (oro apejuwe) that describe nouns and adverbs (oro atokun) that modify verbs, understanding their roles in Yoruba grammar

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Understanding conjunctions (oro asopo) in Yoruba, learning how they connect words, phrases, and sentences to form compound and complex structures

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Batani Ihun Oro - F, KF, FKF
context: Learning syllable patterns in Yoruba words: V (vowel only like 'o, a, e'), CV (consonant-vowel like 'wa, je'), and CVC patterns (like 'eja, owo')

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Understanding three types of Yoruba sentences: simple sentences (alabode), compound sentences (alakanpo), and complex sentences (onibo), with examples of each type

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Reading and analyzing selected Yoruba historical plays, understanding setting (ibudo) where events occur and plot sequence (ahunpo) of the story

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Ere Onitan - Eda Itan ati Ifiwaweda
context: Studying characters (eda itan) in historical plays and analyzing their characterization (ifiwaweda), understanding how characters are portrayed and developed

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Ere Onitan - Koko Oro ati Eko
context: Identifying themes (koko oro) in plays and extracting moral lessons (eko), relating dramatic messages to contemporary societal issues

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Practical drama performance in class, students participate in role-playing scenes from the play to understand character emotions and story progression

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Reading selected Yoruba poems on themes like human behavior, religious/cultural tolerance, women's issues, politics, economics, and environmental concerns

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Asayan Ewi - Ona-ede ati Isowolo Ede
context: Analyzing poetic devices (ona-ede) and figurative language (isowolo ede) in poetry including metaphors (afiwe), proverbs (owe), and idiomatic expressions (akanlo ede)

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Itan Aroso - Isoniṣoki ati Koko Oro
context: Reading selected Yoruba prose fiction, summarizing the story (isonisoki) and identifying major themes (koko oro) that relate to real-life situations

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Itan Aroso - Eda Itan ati Ifiwaweda
context: Analyzing characters in prose narratives, understanding their characterization, personality traits, and how they contribute to the story's development and themes

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Itan Aroso - Ilo Ede ati Asa
context: Examining language use (ilo ede) in prose including literary devices, and identifying cultural practices (asa) reflected in the narrative and their relevance

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Learning traditional Yoruba songs related to weddings (igbeyawo), children's play (eremode), chieftaincy (oye jije), naming ceremonies (ikomjade), and moral instruction (eko iwa rere)

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Understanding Yoruba chants and incantations used in religious worship (ajemesin) and celebrations (ajemayeye), including ijala (hunters' chant), esa/iwi egungun (masquerade chants), iyere ifa (Ifa divination verses)

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Isare - Rara, Ekun Iyawo, Ege, Alamo
context: Learning about different types of Yoruba chants used in celebrations: rara (panegyric chants), ekun iyawo (bride's lament), ege (maiden's songs), and alamo (specific ceremonial chants)

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Ewi Alohun Arangbo - Oriki ati Ese Ifa
context: Understanding full-length traditional poetry (arangbo kikun) including praise poetry (oriki) and Ifa divination verses (ese ifa), their structure and cultural significance

subject: Yoruba Language
topic: Ewi Alohun Arangbo - Owe, Alo Apamo, Aro
context: Learning shorter traditional poetry forms (arangbo kekeke): proverbs (owe), riddles (alo apamo), and curses (aro), understanding their usage in Yoruba communication

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Understanding the importance of religion in Yoruba society, the supreme position of Olodumare (God), and various Yoruba deities (orisa) in traditional religion

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Exploring the relationship between traditional Yoruba religion (esin ibile), Islam (Musulumi), and Christianity (Omoleyin Jesu), understanding religious tolerance and coexistence

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Understanding traditional Yoruba warfare including famous wars (ogun yoruba atijo), warriors (jagunjagun), weapons used, benefits and dangers of war, and ways to promote peace (alaafia)

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Learning about Yoruba naming ceremony (isomoloruko), the procedure, materials used (ireke, oyin, ataare), and types of names: abiso, amutorunwa, oriki, abiku, inagije, idile

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Understanding traditional Yoruba cooperative systems of mutual assistance including esusu (rotating savings), ajo (contribution groups), owe (cooperative farming), aaro (communal labor), and arokodoko

subject: Yoruba Language
context: Exploring modern cooperative societies and how traditional mutual assistance systems have evolved, understanding the role of cooperation in economic development and community building
